figure_tables.caption: SQLite pressure result for RQ3; labels show deadline misses and background throughput.
figure_tables.label: fig:first_page_qos

figure_tables.caption: Capability comparison that defines AEGIS-Q's design gap. ``Policy'' means storage-visible accelerator/QoS/replay policy, not implementation maturity.
figure_tables.label: tab:capability_matrix
figure_tables.obligation: capability table defining the design gap

figure_tables.caption: Motivating boundary for AEGIS-Q. The open systems problem is not encryption alone; it is the combination of authenticated publication, accelerator placement, foreground QoS, and replay-aware recovery in one mounted path.
figure_tables.label: fig:problem_boundary
figure_tables.obligation: problem boundary for the edge-runtime thesis

figure_tables.caption: AEGIS-Q runtime architecture after separating the paper's three boundaries. Solid arrows are implemented mounted paths for foreground reads/writes, publication, recovery, and remount validation; dashed orange arrows are elastic maintenance paths that must pass admission and can fall back to CPU execution. The bottom strip is the claim firewall enforced throughout the paper.
figure_tables.label: fig:overall_procedure

figure_tables.caption: Formal storage-protocol invariants, implementation loci, exercised paths, and claim boundaries.
figure_tables.label: tab:design_goals

figure_tables.caption: D/J/C/xattr publication protocol. D is the durable ciphertext sidecar state, J is the journal mapping state, and C is the authenticated checkpoint/logical-size exposure state. The bottom row is the claim boundary: tails without J are unreachable, stale generations cannot supersede newer mappings, TPM replay-after-advance fails closed, and physical power-loss certification remains outside scope.
figure_tables.label: fig:djc_state_machine

figure_tables.caption: Memory-path hardware claims and their exercised status.
figure_tables.label: tab:memory_compat

figure_tables.caption: Implementation boundaries that are easy to misread without an explicit summary.
figure_tables.label: tab:impl_boundaries

figure_tables.caption: Evaluation summary generated from retained validation JSON. Panel~(a) shows the strict frozen-contract cost boundary for RQ5; Panel~(b) shows mounted SQLite QoS and kernel-control rows for RQ3; Panel~(c) shows the mounted open-file key-plane refresh workflow for RQ2.
figure_tables.label: fig:evaluation_summary
figure_tables.obligation: evaluation spine across cost, QoS, and key-plane placement

figure_tables.caption: SQLite p99 under mounted secure-storage pressure. Hero rows are five-run medians; kernel rows are 3 retained runs each.
figure_tables.label: tab:qos_sqlite_recovery
